title: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)
summary: Spatial feature extraction using local receptive fields and parameter sharing.
key_concepts: Convolution: kernel slides over input, local feature detection
Parameter sharing reduces params vs fully connected
Pooling: max pool, average pool for spatial invariance
Feature maps stack to detect hierarchical features
Classic architectures: LeNet, AlexNet, VGG, ResNet
Residual connections: solve vanishing gradient in deep nets
exercises: Implement 2D convolution from scratch (no loops preferred).
Implement a simple CNN with PyTorch for MNIST classification.
Explain why ResNet residual connections work: F(x) + x.
What is the receptive field of a 3-layer CNN with 3x3 kernels?
code_example:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

class ResidualBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, channels):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1)
        self.bn1 = nn.BatchNorm2d(channels)
        self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1)
        self.bn2 = nn.BatchNorm2d(channels)
        self.relu = nn.ReLU()

    def forward(self, x):
        residual = x
        out = self.relu(self.bn1(self.conv1(x)))
        out = self.bn2(self.conv2(out))
        return self.relu(out + residual)  # skip connection

class SimpleCNN(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_classes=10):
        super().__init__()
        self.features = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(1, 32, 3, padding=1), nn.ReLU(), nn.MaxPool2d(2),
            ResidualBlock(32),
            nn.Conv2d(32, 64, 3, padding=1), nn.ReLU(), nn.MaxPool2d(2)
        )
        self.classifier = nn.Linear(64*7*7, num_classes)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.features(x)
        return self.classifier(x.flatten(1))
thinking_questions: Why does parameter sharing in CNNs make sense for image data?
What problem do residual connections solve and why mathematically?
